text: Battery X Metals Announces Closing of First Tranche of Private Placement to Advance Corporate Growth. VANCOUVER, BC / ACCESS Newswire / June 5, 2026 / Battery X Metals Inc. (CSE:BATX)(OTCQB:BATXF)(FSE:5YW0, WKN:A41RJF) ("Battery X Metals" or the "Company") an energy transition resource exploration and technology company, announces that, further to its news release dated May 22, 2026, the Company has closed the first tranche (the "First Tranche") of its previously announced non-brokered private placement (the "Private Placement"), through the issuance of 218,182 units of the Company (each, a "Unit") at a price of $2.75 per Unit, for aggregate gross proceeds of $600,000.50. Each Unit consists of one common share in the capital of the Company (each, a "Share") and one transferable common share purchase warrant (each, a "Warrant").
label: neutral

text: Revisiting The SpaceX Valuation: A Post-Prospectus Update. I will use this post to talk about the information gained by having access to a company's financials and why the information you glean from those financials is different at younger companies, with growth potential, relative to mature companies. In my pre-prospectus valuation, where I assessed the value of SpaceX at roughly $1.2 trillion, I relied on scraps of information, including leaked stories of estimated revenues ($15.5 billion) and EBITDA of $8 billion, since I did not have access to the company's full financial statements. SpaceX has the largest TAM of any company in history, with a total TAM of $28 trillion, and AI accounts for $26 trillion of that market estimate.
